reasoning: The bill has advanced through the Knesset National Security Committee (approved March 24, 2026) and is cleared for its second and third readings. The sponsor party, Otzma Yehudit, claimed the final votes are expected 'next week' (March 29-31). Since the Knesset begins its Passover recess shortly and must pass the 2026 State Budget by March 31, there will be intense, marathon voting sessions during this three-day window. While the coalition has the majority to pass it and Ben-Gvir is highly motivated, the tight window poses risks. The state budget will consume massive floor time with opposition filibusters and amendments. It's common for controversial bills not tied directly to the budget to be delayed until after recess unless Otzma Yehudit has issued a hard ultimatum blocking the budget over this bill. Given these competing dynamics, the probability of passage specifically before the April 1, 2026 UTC deadline is higher than the baseline market of 40% due to the committee's recent actions, but constrained by legislative congestion, settling at around 65%.
